prose:
**Water frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO orbital gap. Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` with explicitly requested **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** using PySCF; density fitting was not requested, so this was the plain PySCF DFT path. Geometry: fixed, user-supplied coordinates from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz` (3 atoms); this frontier-orbitals task is a single-point orbital calculation, not a geometry optimization. Environment: **gas phase** (`solvent: null`). Charge 0, multiplicity 1.

Headline value: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV**.

Additional returned orbital quantities: HOMO = -8.6037183 eV; LUMO = 0.33695037 eV. Total electronic energy = -76.462901 hartree = -2080.6615 eV. SCF converged: true; SCF cycles: 7. Integrity trustworthy verdict: **true**.

Warnings: no warnings were reported by the tool result.

Reproducibility: chemkit command returned by the engine was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmps9xatn9m/live.json`. The result JSON path in that invocation is `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmps9xatn9m/live.json`. I announced the live `.out` log generically when the run was launched, but the chemkit tool response did not expose the exact timestamped `.out` path, so I will not fabricate one.
